transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes
What is your main medical concern today?
I have hair loss.
When did you first notice the hair loss?
It's been
Can you tell me when exactly you first noticed the hair loss?
falling out more and more, it seems, over the last 6 months.
Can you describe where on your scalp or body the hair loss is occurring?
Over the
Can you finish describing where on your scalp or body the hair loss is occurring?
I it's on the my head. It is on my head.
Can you describe the pattern of hair loss on your head, such as thinning on the top, receding hairline, or patchy areas?
Sitting on the top.
Have you noticed any itching, redness, pain, or scaling on your scalp where the hair is thinning?
No.
Have you experienced any recent significant stress, illness, or changes in your diet or medications in the past 6 months?
No.
Have you noticed any other symptoms such as weight changes, fatigue, or changes in your menstrual cycle?
My period is getting further and further apart because I'm perimenopausal, I guess.
Any family history of hair loss or thinning, especially on the top of the head?
My mother says her hair is thinning.
Have you tried any treatments or remedies for your hair loss so far?
No.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
No.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Male
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No

Based on what you’ve described, your hair loss most likely fits a common pattern called androgenetic alopecia, which tends to run in families and progresses gradually. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or do tests, but a licensed physician will review your information and may recommend starting a topical medication like minoxidil to help slow hair loss. If you notice any sudden or rapid hair loss, scalp pain, redness, or signs of infection, please seek in-person care urgently. Otherwise, you can consider supportive care and monitor your symptoms. A physician will review your case and decide if medication is appropriate. If you need an in-person exam or testing, please go to an urgent care or see your primary care clinician, as this service does not include in-person visits.
